subject.title: Spanish B
subject.description:
**IB Spanish B** is a **Group 2: Language Acquisition** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **five prescribed themes: Identities, Experiences, Human ingenuity, Social organisation, and Sharing the planet**, with a strong focus on **developing receptive (listening and reading), productive (writing), and interactive (speaking) language skills while fostering intercultural understanding through exploration of Spanish-speaking cultures**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2020**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Writing task (choose 1 of 3 options) | 1h 15m | 1h 30m | 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Reading and listening comprehension | 1h 45m | 2h | 50% |
| **Individual Oral (IA)** | Oral presentation and discussion | 15 minutes | 15 minutes | 25% |

strengths: Clear, focused communication of the topic throughout with well-labelled sections that maintain reader orientation
Explicitly stated, narrowly framed research question that ties directly back to each major discussion point
weaknesses: No standalone methodology section explaining source selection criteria
Occasional minor digressions into broader Peruvian history dilute the strict methodological focus
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the methodology description is not fully explicit, the topic is communicated accurately and effectively, and the research question is clearly stated, sharply focused and sustained throughout. The completeness of planning (bibliography, citations, word count) and the seamless integration of purpose and structure meet the topâband descriptors for Focus and Method.

strengths: Mostly relevant and appropriate application of quotations, statistics and historical facts to support each line of argument
Sound, coherent subject knowledge of culinary history with accurate discussion of key figures and signature dishes
weaknesses: Frequent spelling and accentuation errors undermine professional presentation of terminology
Some citations are incomplete or lack full bibliographic detail
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student applies source material in a generally relevant way and demonstrates clear knowledge of the subject. However, inconsistent use of terminology and occasional citation inaccuracies keep performance squarely in the mid-band for Knowledge and Understanding.

strengths: Wide range of pertinent sources consistently tied back to the research question
Analysis is coherent and conclusions are well supported by evidence
Discussion is focused and delivers a cohesive, balanced argument with clear evaluation of competing influences
weaknesses: Limited explicit evaluation of source reliability and causation versus correlation issues
A few general assertions would benefit from additional quantitative substantiation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation demonstrates excellent critical thinking: research is consistently relevant, analysis is thorough and supports convincing conclusions, and the discussion is tightly reasoned and coherent. Minor lapses do not detract from the overall excellence of argumentation and evaluation, justifying a topâband score.

strengths: Report structure is fully appropriate, with clear introduction, logically ordered headings, conclusion and correctly numbered contents
Consistent formatting, clear font choice, complete title page and word count
weaknesses: Some headings truncate key terminology, interrupting visual clarity
A few image labels are cramped and sentence fragments appear in body text
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation is highly effective overallâstructure and layout meet topâband criteria. Minor formatting issues with truncated headings and isolated sentence fragments do not materially impede navigation or readability, warranting the maximum score for Presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicitly formulated on the title page, is narrowly framed around two measurable dimensions (gastronomy development/popularisation and tourism influence) and is repeatedly cited in the essay.  Links between the question and subsequent discussion are **direct and sustained**, though the dual focus does lead to moments of dilution.  Hence a secure, but not flawless, placement at 5.

The student moves beyond narrative by comparing Chinese vs. Japanese influence and by linking festival timelines to tourism revenue, but analysis often stops at assertion (e.g., "inmigraciÃ³n oriental fue inmensa" without quantified evidence).  Logical flow is present yet **depth is uneven and occasionally anecdotal**, placing it at the lower end of 4-6.

Quotations, statistics and historical facts from listed references are woven into the narrative of each subsection and generally support the line of argument.  While some use is descriptive and a few citations are incomplete, most material is **relevant and appropriately applied** to illustrate immigration waves, dish evolution and tourism data, meriting the middle band.

Title page, word count, page numbers and consistent font are evident.  Minor issues (some truncated headings, image labels cramped) prevent a perfect score but **layout elements are generally correct and complete**, earning 3 within 3-4.

The student demonstrates sound knowledge of Peruvian, Chinese and Japanese culinary history, referencing key figures (Wong, Tsumura) and signature dishes with accuracy.  The narrative is coherent, yet some historical details (e.g., Taiping emperor) are superficial and sourced from videos rather than academic texts, so the use of knowledge is **partially but not fully effective**.

A clear introduction, sequential headings, conclusion, bibliography and appendices give the essay a **fully appropriate structure**.  Transitions are logical and the table of contents matches page numbers, satisfying top-band descriptors.

An array of sources (historical records, media articles, tourism statistics) is assembled and generally pertinent to the question.  While not exhaustive and skewed toward popular media, the research base is **adequate and shows clear relevance** throughout, justifying a mid-band 5 within 4-6.

The student states a precise cultural topicâthe impact of Chinese and Japanese immigration on Peruvian fusion cuisine and tourismâand maintains this focus through clearly labelled sections.  Although some subsections drift slightly into general Peruvian history, the overall purpose (to gauge the significance of that immigration) remains **consistently visible and appropriate**, satisfying the top band descriptors, but occasional digressions keep the quality to the lower end of 5-6.

The essay offers a structured argument culminating in a balanced conclusion that weighs Nikkei vs. Chifa impacts.  Nevertheless, evaluation of limitations (e.g., source reliability, causation vs. correlation in tourism) is minimal, and some claims lack explicit evidence, so **reasoned discussion is present but not consistently thorough**.

A bibliography, word count, table of contents and in-text citations are present, indicating planning.  The essay names key primary/secondary sources (academic article, news sites, You-Tube documentaries, Statista data) and comments briefly on how they will be used.  However, there is **no explicit methodology section explaining criteria for source selection or data treatment**, and many sources are journalistic rather than scholarly, so evidence of informed selection is only partial.

Key culinary terms such as *Nikkei*, *Chifa*, *tiradito* and *ceviche* are used correctly, but there are frequent spelling/accents errors ("inmigraci", "integraci") and some conceptual imprecision (confusing âbiodiversityâ with culinary resources).  Terminology is therefore **sometimes accurate, sometimes flawed**, aligning with the 1-2 band ceiling.

annotations.comment: The research question is stated prominently with clear focus on assessing Chinese and Japanese immigration impacts on both gastronomy development and tourism, providing coherent direction for the investigation.
The title succinctly captures the cultural phenomenon under study, reinforcing topical focus on Peruâs gastronomic boom influenced by Eastern immigration.
Consider adding a standalone methodology section explaining criteria for source selection and data treatment to demonstrate an informed and transparent methodological approach.
Heading truncation âIntroducciâ disrupts section clarity; ensure full headings appear consistently to maintain professional layout.
The example of Chinese cooks joining the Peruvian army effectively illustrates cultural integration and its culinary impact.
Section heading âEl desarrollo de la integraciâ truncates key term âintegraciÃ³nâ; correct all truncated headings for consistency.
Statement âEl impacto de la inmigraciÃ³n oriental fue inmensoâ needs supporting data; integrate quantitative evidence or statistics to substantiate such claims.
The comparison between tiradito and sashimi techniques is a clear conceptual explanation of fusion cuisine, enhancing reader understanding.
Section header âEl boom de la gastronâ truncates âgastronÃ³micaâ; revise formatting to ensure full headings are displayed.
Contextualizing the boom within Peruâs social and political history deepens the analysis by linking culinary trends to broader national developments.
The table of contents includes all major sections but blank cells cause misalignment and visual confusion. Refine cell formatting to enhance navigational clarity.
In the introduction, explicitly link the tourism dimension to the research question by outlining how fusion cuisine influenced visitor numbers and experiences.
Paragraph ends abruptly at âfue creado paraâ; complete this sentence to preserve narrative flow and clarity.
Citation for Margorie (2020) lacks page number and full bibliographic detail; include complete reference information to strengthen academic rigor.
Reference âAsia con Fleguilloâ is missing publication date and context; provide full citation details to ensure source traceability.
Reliance on personal communications (A. Alarcon) requires critical evaluation; corroborate these with published data or explain their reliability.
Visitor numbers from the âPerÃº, Mucho Gustoâ fair are compelling but lack a primary data citation; reference the original statistics source to strengthen evidence.
Chef Mitsuharu Tsumuraâs surname is misspelled; verify proper names and correct all references for accuracy.
Phrase âhaya: âmuchos restauradoresâ¦â is grammatically awkward; rephrase this sentence to improve readability and coherence.
Incorporate actual tourism statistics or caseâstudy data to substantiate claims about the impact of fusion cuisine festivals on visitor numbers.
Ensure retrieval date and access details are included for online data sources like Statista forecasts to enhance credibility and traceability.
Bibliographic entries show inconsistent formatting and missing italics; adopt one citation style (APA or MLA) and apply it uniformly throughout.
